data.title: Bernstein starts Melexis with 'outperform', says auto sensors drive growth
data.description:
** Bernstein initiates Belgian chipmaker Melexis with "outperform" rating and target price of 73 euros, expecting structural growth from automotive electrification and sensor demand

** The company holds a niche position in automotive sensors and drivers, the broker says, adding that cars require more electronic content for electric vehicles, advanced safety systems and smart features

** Bernstein sees Melexis' addressable market growing 7% annually over the next five years, driven by higher electronic content per vehicle even with flat car production volumes

** The broker says cyclical recovery is underway, with automotive demand having bottomed 6-9 months ago and Melexis on track to surpass its 2023 peak revenue by 2027

** It highlights Melexis' focus on "safety-critical, hard-to-displace applications" and strong relationships with European and Asian automakers as key competitive advantages

** Out of 14 analysts that cover Melexis, eight rate it "strong buy" or "buy", ​five "hold" and one "sell" - LSEG data

** Shares of Melexis rise 1.8%, paring earlier gains of 5.3%

data.title: Bank Of America Corporation Q4 Sales Increase
data.description:
CHARLOTTE (dpa-AFX) - Bank of America Corporation (BAC) announced a profit for fourth quarter of $7.319 billion

The company's earnings came in at $7.319 billion, or $0.98 per share. This compares with $7.738 billion, or $0.83 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 7.1% to $28.367 billion from $26.475 billion last year.

Bank of America Corporation earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $7.319 Bln. vs. $7.738 Bln. last year.

-EPS: $0.98 vs. $0.83 last year.

-Revenue: $28.367 Bln vs. $26.475 Bln last year.

data.title: Japanese companies lead on climate leadership, CDP data shows
data.description:
Key points:
  • 22% of companies achieving 'climate leadership'
  • Helped by more companies getting SBTi sign-off
  • Corporates still pushing ahead despite politics, CEO says

By Sharon Kimathi

Japanese companies have topped an annual ranking of corporate climate efforts in part because more have now had their targets signed off by a leading independent validator, non-profit data tracker CDP said on Wednesday.

Japan took the lead with 22% of companies achieving what CDP defined as "climate leadership", followed by the UK with 17%, the European Union with 16%, and China and Southeast Asia at 8%, it said in a report, co-authored with consultants Oliver Wyman.

As the world's only independent environmental disclosure system, CDP assesses over 10,000 companies on their awareness of environmental issues, management practices, transparency and performance.

It also looks at whether targets have been signed off by the Science-Based Targets Initiative (SBTi), a leading independent standard setter - something that had helped the Japanese firms outperform, CDP Chief Executive Sherry Madera said.

Now in their second year, the rankings showed that global companies were still prioritising sustainability, despite geopolitical and economic uncertainty and a recent wave of climate regulatory rollbacks in the U.S. and Europe, she said.

Companies were also assessed on climate, water and forest performance, which covers emissions and climate strategy, water use and risk management, and deforestation risks in key commodities, such as palm oil, soy, timber and cattle.

The report found that all of the top-performing companies on climate and most of those leading on the topics of water and forest tied executive pay to environmental goals.

“Maybe companies are becoming quieter when celebrating their wins in the market, but they are still working towards sustainability, and year-on-year, leaders in the Corporate Health Check are the ones who link their executive pay to climate leadership and that trend has solidified," said Madera.

data.title: These Stocks Are Today's Movers: Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Tesla, Netflix, Nvidia, Fortinet, Rivian, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Stock futures were falling Wednesday as investors awaited quarterly earnings from major U.S. banks, a batch of economic data, and a potential Supreme Court ruling on President Donald Trump's tariffs.

These stocks were poised to make moves:

Bank of America rose 0.1%, Citigroup ticked up 0.2%, and Wells Fargo slid 0.4% ahead of the opening bell. The three banks are scheduled to report their fourth-quarter earnings Wednesday, and the results will be in focus for investors seeing as rival JPMorgan Chase's 7% drop in profit dragged down the broader market on Tuesday.

Netflix rose 1.3% in the premarket session. The video streamer is considering making its offer for Warner Bros. Discovery's streaming and studios assets all-cash, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Netflix is trying to get the deal done quickly as rival Paramount Skydance carries on pushing its competing bid. Netflix declined to comment on the possible revised offer. Warner Bros. Discovery fell 0.7%.

Nvidia slipped 0.7%. The chip maker got approval from U.S. authorities to ship its H200 artificial-intelligence chips to China, but it still faces an anxious wait to see if Beijing will allow sales to go ahead. Chinese companies would be willing to buy around 1.5 million of the H200 chips, representing roughly $30 billion in revenue, according to KeyBanc analyst John Vinh.

Fortinet dropped 2.3% and Palo Alto Networks fell 3.3%, making them among the S&P 500's two biggest losers ahead of the opening bell. Chinese authorities have told local companies to stop using cybersecurity software made by a dozen U.S. and Israeli companies, Reuters reported, citing two people briefed on the matter. Fortinet and Palo Alto Networks didn't respond to requests for comment from Barron's.

Intel was the biggest gainer, climbing 2.3%. The chip maker has benefited from the AI rally lately and it caught an upgrade from KeyBanc's Vinh on Monday, who cited strong demand from cloud service providers.

Rivian Automotive dropped 3.5% to $18.19 after UBS downgraded the electric-vehicle maker to Sell from Neutral even as it raised its price target to $15 from $13. Analysts said that the market's appetite for Rivian's AI efforts may have peaked, following a 15% rally for the stock since Dec. 11.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Bank of America's Profit Climbs as Consumer Spending Rises — WSJ
data.description:
By Alexander Saeedy

Bank of America said its profit rose 12% in the fourth quarter of last year, boosted by stronger revenue from its consumer bank and its sales and trading arm.

The bank said it earned $7.6 billion, or 98 cents per share. Analysts expected earnings of 96 cents per share. Full-year profit was $30.51 billion, up 13% versus last year.

Revenue for the fourth quarter rose to $28.37 billion. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $27.76 billion.

Consumer health appeared steady, with delinquencies of over 90 days on credit cards down 1.27% versus 1.35% a year ago. Card spending rose 6% from last year.

"With consumers and businesses proving resilient, as well as the regulatory environment and tax and trade policies coming into sharper focus, we expect further economic growth in the year ahead," CEO Brian Moynihan said in a press release. "We are bullish on the U.S. economy in 2026."

The country's biggest banks are reporting their quarterly financial results this week, offering a snapshot of a U.S. economy that has been contending with President Trump's tariffs, persistent inflation and weakening job prospects.

On Tuesday, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said consumers were still spending and that businesses remained healthy, though he warned of risks from geopolitical conditions. The bank reported higher spending on credit and debit cards, and said the over 90-day delinquency rate on credit cards edged lower.

data.title: SLAM Cuts High Grade Gold & Silver in Channel Samples
data.description:
Gold, Silver and Critical Elements in the Mineral-Rich Province of New Brunswick, Canada

MIRAMICHI, NB / ACCESS Newswire / January 14, 2026 / SLAM Exploration Ltd. (TSXV:SXL) ("SLAM" or the "Company")is pleased to report new high grade assays up to 40.5 grams per tonne ("g/t") gold and 63.3 g/t silver from channel samples sawn across the No. 1 vein on its wholly owned Jake Lee claims. The Jake Lee project is located 25 kilometers southeast of the Clarence Stream gold deposit operated by Galway Metals Inc. in New Brunswick, Canada.

Three channels were cut across the No. 1 vein and extended into the wall rock on both sides of the vein. The three channel samples cut across the vein assayed 31.3 g/t gold, 12.3 g/t gold and 40.5 g/t gold along with 63.30 g/t silver, 23.2 g/t silver and 25.1 g/t silver as summarized in the following table:

The first 3 samples in the table were cut from the No. 1 vein. The other 8 samples were sawn from wall rock on both sides of the vein. All of the wall rock samples contain gold ranging from 0.118 g/t to 0.898 g/t gold.

Figure 1: The No. 1 Vein

QA-QC Procedures: After cleaning and washing, 3 channels were sawn across the No. 1 vein and into the wallrock. Channel JLCS2 and channel JLCS3 were spaced 3 and 4 meters respectively meters east of channel JLCS1. Eleven channel samples were collected and delivered directly to Actlabs in Fredericton, New Brunswick for analysis. Actlabs analyzed all the samples for gold using procedure 1A2 (fire assay with atomic absorption finish) and 1A3 (gravimetric finish) for over-limit samples. Actlabs also used the ICP 1E3 method to analyze the samples for multiple elements

Jake Lee Project Update

Channel sample assays range up to 40.50 g/t gold and 63.30 g/t silver in the No. 1 gold vein as reported above from SLAM's wholly-owned Jake Lee claims. The Company had previously reported eight grab samples with assays ranging from 7.42 grams per tonne ("g/t") to 94.80 g/t gold from the No. 1 gold vein on July 9, 2025. A second discovery comprised quartz float grading 16.20 and 3.78 g/t gold respectively 100 meters southwest of the initial discovery as reported August 28, 2025.

The Jake Lee claims are located 25 kilometers southeast of the Clarence Stream gold deposit where Galway Metals Inc. Clarence Stream is host to a 12.4M tonne indicated resource of 922,000 ounces at a grade of 2.31 g/t gold plus an inferred resource of 16.1m tonnes with 1,334,000 ounces at a grade of 2.60 g/t gold. (Reference: "Updated Mineral Resource Statement, Clarence Stream Deposits, New Brunswick, Canada, by SLR Consulting (Canada) Ltd., March 31, 2022"). SLAM's exploration team recently mobilized back to Jake Lee.

Historic Menneval Results

SLAM previously reported gold bearing core intervals from the Maisie vein, including 162.5 g/t gold over 0.2 m and 56.90 g/t gold over 0.5 m, in news releases dated December 13, 2021 and November 22, 2022. The Company also reported a core interval grading 3,955 g/t (127oz) gold over 0.1m from the No. 18 vein. The Menneval soil geochemical survey shows a number of gold anomalies over an area measuring approximately 3,000 meters by 2,500 meters.

About SLAM Exploration Ltd: SLAM Exploration Ltd. is a publicly listed resource company with a 40,000-hectare portfolio of mineral claim holdings in the mineral-rich province of New Brunswick. This portfolio is built around the Goodwin Copper Nickel Cobalt project in the Bathurst Mining Camp ("BMC") of New Brunswick. The Company drilled 10 holes in the 2025 diamond drilling campaign on the Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project. This followed significant copper, nickel and cobalt intercepts from 15 diamond drill holes reported by the Company in 2024. These include a 64.90 meter core interval, grading 2.19% Cu-Eq (copper-nickel-cobalt), including 3.84% Cu-Eq over a 31.20 meter core interval from hole GW24-02 as reported in a news release August 7, 2024. Significant gold values were also reported with up to 3.31 grams per tonne over 0.5m in hole GW24-01.

The Company is a project generator and expects to receive significant cash and share payments in 2026. SLAM received 1,200,000 shares plus cash from Nine Mile Metals Inc. (NINE) in 2025 pursuant to the Wedge project agreement. Also in 2025, the Company received a cash payment of $60,000 as well as 180,000 shares of a private company pursuant to the Ramsay gold agreement. The Company holds NSR royalties and expects to receive additional cash and share payments on the Wedge copper zinc project and on the Ramsay gold project.

To view SLAM's corporate presentation, click SXL-Presentation. Additional information is available on SLAM's website and on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. Follow us on X @SLAMGold.Join our company newsletter by clicking SXL-News to receive timely company updates and press releases relating to SLAM Exploration.

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: ScienceSoft to present a multi-agent AI taking most contact center tasks at WHX Dubai 2026
data.description:
FUJAIRAH, United Arab Emirates--(BUSINESS WIRE/AETOSWire)-- ScienceSoft will speak at the World Health Expo (WHX) Dubai 2026, the event formerly known as Arab Health. On February 11, 2026, at 14:30 (UTC+4), Hadeel Abu Baker, Senior Healthcare IT Consultant at ScienceSoft, will speak at the Dubai Exhibition Centre in Expo City Dubai.

Her session, “AI Agents First: A New Operating Model for Healthcare Contact Centers,” explains how a multi-agent AI can handle most contact center calls, while staff focus on verification, escalation, and outcome control. It focuses on real-world call complexity, from handling Arabic dialects to dealing with complex multi-intent requests (e.g., rescheduling, location and parking guidance, and payment questions all within one interaction).

Our expert will cover:

  • A multi-agent design where a router assigns tasks to specialist agents and controls context handoff, so the patient is not asked the same questions twice.
  • The functional architecture, including routing and integrations with scheduling and CRM.
  • The guardrails that keep it safe, including identity checks, escalation rules, and do-not-answer boundaries.
  • The KPIs used to measure the agent’s impact.

The attendees will also see a live demo featuring an agent that switches from UAE Arabic to English upon request, handles long, multi-intent conversation turns, and completes voice flows with WhatsApp follow-ups.

About WHX Dubai 2026

The World Health Expo (WHX) Dubai, formerly known as Arab Health, is recognized as the largest healthcare trade show and medical conference in Dubai. It’s presented as a forum for global leaders, suppliers, and innovators to connect and do business across the healthcare ecosystem. For 2026, the organizers cite more than 235,000 professional visits, over 4,300 exhibitors, and representation from more than 180 countries. The program includes six CME-accredited conferences focused on clinical excellence, digital transformation, and surgical innovation.

Need Insider Healthcare AI Insights?

ScienceSoft’s experts share practice-based commentary on healthcare AI for healthcare and MedTech media. We provide interview-ready quotes, briefing notes, and anonymized case examples of using governed AI agents for healthcare workflows. We can also explain adoption risks, privacy boundaries, and KPI design with a Gulf region lens. Visit our Press Room to request an expert opinion or to arrange a short interview at WHX Dubai 2026.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Wells Fargo Q4 Net Income Rises; Revenue Up 4%
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (dpa-AFX) - Wells Fargo (WFC) reported that its fourth quarter net income increased to $5.36 billion from $5.08 billion, a year ago. Earnings per common share was $1.62 compared to 1.43. Net income, excluding severance expense, was $5.8 billion, or $1.76 per share, for the quarter.

Fourth quarter total revenue increased to $21.29 billion from $20.38 billion, prior year. Net interest income increased to $12.33 billion from $11.84 billion. Net interest income increased 4%, driven by higher loan and investment securities balances, improved results in Markets business, and fixed rate asset repricing, partially offset by deposit mix changes. Noninterest income increased 5%.

data.title: Honeywell's Quantinuum Quantum-Computing Unit Plans IPO
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Honeywell's majority-owned Quantinuum quantum-computing unit is planning to go public.

Honeywell on Wednesday said Quantinuum plans to make a confidential submission of a draft registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a proposed initial public offering.

Honeywell in September said Quantinuum had raised $600 million at a pre-money equity valuation of $10 billion, with chip maker Nvidia joining as a new investor.

Honeywell formed Quantinuum in 2021 by merging its own quantum unit with Cambridge Quantum.

The Charlotte, N.C., industrial conglomerate is in the process of carving itself into three publicly traded companies. Honeywell in October completed the spinoff of its advanced-materials business as Solstice Advanced Materials, and the company expects to complete the spinoff of its aerospace-technologies arm later this year.

Honeywell said the number of Quantinuum shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering haven't yet been determined.

Quantinuum's planned filing won't be visible to the public because the company will be taking advantage of a commonly used confidential-filing provision introduced in 2012 as part of the Jumpstart Our Business Startups Act.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: Wall St futures edge lower as Bank of America, Wells Fargo earnings roll in
data.description:
Key points:
  • Midcap, smallcap indexes outperform S&P 500 so far in 2026
  • Fed speeches may provide clues on rate trajectory
  • Futures down: Dow 0.31%, S&P 500 0.4%, Nasdaq 0.59%

By Medha Singh and Pranav Kashyap

U.S. stock index futures edged lower on Wednesday as investors digested earnings from Bank of America and Wells Fargo, with retail sales and producer price data due later in the day poised to deliver a fresh read on the economy's pulse.

Shares of Bank of America edged 0.5% higher after reporting a higher profit, as its traders brought in more revenue from a spurt in client activity in the fourth quarter.

Wells Fargo slipped 2% after missing fourth-quarter revenue estimates, while Citigroup edged lower ahead of its results. On Monday, JPMorgan executives warned that a proposed ceiling on credit-card interest rates could squeeze consumers and dent profitability across the financial sector.

While Wall Street's deal desks are expected to benefit from revived dealmaking in the final quarter, investors are also looking for signals on credit quality and consumer sentiment.

Wall Street's main indexes have stalled this week after the Dow and the S&P 500 touched record highs, with traders awaiting fourth-quarter earnings to see if they meet elevated expectations.

Analysts project S&P 500 companies will deliver average fourth-quarter earnings growth of 8.8% year-on-year, lifting full-year 2025 bottom-line expansion to 13.2%, according to IBES LSEG data.

At 6:47 a.m. ET, Dow E-minis (YMcv1) were down 153 points, or 0.31%, S&P 500 E-minis were down 28.25 points, or 0.4% and Nasdaq 100 E-minis were down 153.75 points, or 0.59%.

PRODUCER PRICES AND RETAIL TESTS LOOM

After December's expected consumer-price rise, investors are now watching Wednesday's producer price and retail sales releases, alongside speeches from Fed voting members John Williams, Anna Paulson, Stephen Miran and Neel Kashkari.

For now, interest rates are widely expected to hold steady through the first half of the year, including at the Fed's January meeting, as long as inflation and growth remain on track. Traders, however, are still pricing in at least two cuts before year-end, according to LSEG data.

"PPI will hold clues on CPI risks," said Elias Haddad, global head of markets strategy at Brown Brothers Harriman.

"Progress towards the Fed's 2% inflation goal is stalling. But upside risks to prices are fading."

Meanwhile, the Danish and Greenlandic foreign ministers will meet with U.S. Vice President JD Vance at the White House later in the day following weeks of threats by President Donald Trump to take control of Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark.

So far, markets have largely overlooked geopolitical risks such as the U.S. toppling Venezuela's leader, with enthusiasm around artificial intelligence and fourth-quarter results driving U.S. indexes to new peaks.

The stock market's record rally is also showing early signs of broadening out this year, with S&P 400 midcaps and S&P 600 smallcaps (.SPCY) outperforming the largecap S&P 500 so far this year.

Palo Alto Networks and Fortinet fell 3.3% and 2.5%, respectively, before the bell after sources told Reuters that Chinese authorities have told domestic companies to stop using some U.S. and Israeli cybersecurity software due to national security concerns.

data.title: Honeywell announces Quantinuum's plan to file for IPO
data.description:
Honeywell said on Wednesday it plans for its majority-owned quantum computing unit Quantinuum to confidentially file draft IPO papers with the U.S. securities regulator.

The number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined, the company said.

Following the news, Honeywell's shares were up about 1% during premarket trading.

Last year, Quantinuum raised about $600 million from investors, including Nvidia's venture capital arm, at a valuation of $10 billion.

data.title: Bel Fuse Is Maintained at Buy by Needham
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: US Futures Lower as Bank Earnings Kick In
data.description:
US stock futures were lower on Wednesday, with S&P 500 futures down 0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures slipping 0.5%, and Dow Jones futures falling nearly 120 points, as traders digested another round of bank earnings.

Shares of Wells Fargo declined 1.6% in premarket trading after the bank missed earnings and profit expectations, while Bank of America rose 1.8% after beating forecasts for both earnings and revenue.

Citigroup slipped about 0.3% ahead of its results, due before the opening bell.

JPMorgan edged up 0.1% after plunging 4.1% in the previous session, when its quarterly results disappointed investors.

Meanwhile, traders are bracing for today’s PPI report, which is expected to provide further insight into inflationary pressures in October and November.

Retail sales data will also be closely watched for signals on consumer strength.

In addition, investors continue to monitor developments in Iran, while the US Supreme Court could rule on tariffs today.

data.title: HSBC Holdings Reportedly Reviewing Options For Singapore Insurance Business
data.description:
LONDON (dpa-AFX) - HSBC Holdings Plc (HSBC) is reviewing strategic options for its insurance business in Singapore, including a potential sale, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

According to the report, HSBC and a financial adviser are working on a review of HSBC Life (Singapore) Pte, which could be valued at more than $1 billion in a transaction. The people said other insurers and investment firms have shown early interest in the unit.

The move follows business revamps undertaken globally under Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: US Mortgage Applications Surge
data.description:
The volume of mortgage applications in the US surged by 28.5% from the previous week in the second full week of January, rebounding from three consecutive periods of declines that were loosely held at the turn of the year, according to data compiled by the Mortgage Bankers Association.

It was the sharpest increase since September, aligned with the decline in benchmark mortgage rates, with that of a 30-year contract dropping to a 15-month low of 6.18%, despite some stubbornness in long-dated Treasury yields.

Applications for contracts to refinance a mortgage, which are sensitive to short-term changes in interest rates, surged by 40% from the previous week.

Meanwhile, applications for a mortgage to purchase a home rose by 16.9%.

data.title: Moldova Trade Deficit Widens in November
data.description:
Moldova recorded a trade deficit of USD 583.3 million in November 2025, up sharply from USD 478.4 million in the same month last year, as imports outpaced exports.

Imports rose 20.3% year-on-year to USD 979.2 million, driven by higher purchases from the EU (up 28.0%), CIS countries (up 3.9%), and other regions (up 12.6%).

Exports grew 18.0% to USD 395.9 million, supported by increased sales to CIS countries (up 13.4%), the EU (up 8.8%), and other countries (up 52.0%).

For the January–November period, Moldova’s trade deficit expanded to USD 6,392.0 million, compared with USD 4,934.7 million in the same period last year.

data.title: P20260114 - PayPal - Lasting Cash Cow Model Disconnected From It's Actual Value
data.description:
Investment Thesis

PayPal's business quality varies significantly by segment, and honest assessment requires acknowledging where defensibility is strong versus under pressure. The branded checkout and consumer wallet (PayPal button, peer-to-peer) remain structurally defensible through network effects and consumer trust180 million active accounts create switching costs and merchant acceptance. Unbranded processing (Braintree) is increasingly commodity-like with compressed margins, but generates massive volume that feeds higher-margin services. The fastest-growing segmentsbuy-now-pay-later, merchant services, value-added servicescommand premium economics and are where PayPal's future profitability concentrates.

The risk is that core payments become utility infrastructure while adjacent services face intense competition from Block, Affirm, and banking incumbents. I'm willing to concede unbranded checkout offers limited pricing power, but the bundled nature of PayPal's ecosystemwhere merchant cash advance customers also use fraud tools and BNPLcreates stickiness that pure-play competitors struggle to replicate.

Stock Chart 3-Year Performance

The 3-year comparisons shows a clear outperformance for FINX as it grew 76.53% in 3 years compared to the negative growth of 12.36% for PYPL. The chart is to a degree flawed as it was valued at a very high level during the years before that and in 2022. PYPLs 5-year Monthly beta is also 1.42 making it a very volatile asset to own. It's still lower than FINX which has a beta of 1.62 instead. This begs the question why the company has such a poor stock chart. I'll dive into this more just below here.

Overstated Pessimism - Now Just Plainly Unfounded

Since the bubble burst in 2021 along with many other high-growth tech stocks the market has done nothing other than award PayPal with a low valuation multiple, expecting little to no growth and in general a great deal of pessimism. That the stock was overvalued then is definitely a valid argument, but now? Not so much.

Paypal has undergone a shift in management with the introduction in 2023 of the new CEO, Alex Chriss. Since then, the company has continued to improve on margins and cut down on operating expenses. Since 2021, operating income has improved greatly, going from $3.22 billion in Q3 2020 to $6.05 billion in Q2 2025. When Alex joined the company as CEO it was in a decline, with consecutive quarters of declining operating income.

To still see such a low multiple applied seems to neglect all of the previous work and changes PayPal has undergone as a company.

Investments into AI solutions will likely lead to further cost improvements and margin expansions. Paypal Ventures, which is the part of the company investing in AI has made since its inception crucial capital investments in the likes of Rasa, Bureau and Finmo. These startups cover areas like fraud prevention to corporate treasury solutions. I will monitor carefully how these investments generate higher margins across the business. These assets are found under strategic investments as explained in the latest SEC filing from PayPal:

Our strategic investments include marketable equity securities, which are publicly traded, and non-marketable equity securities, which are primarily investments in privately held companies.

PayPal incurred a net gain of $59 million on strategic investments during the first months of 2025, marking a change from 2024 when it recorded a $55 million loss instead. Strategic investments are found under the long-term investments, valued currently at $1.576 billion, down from $1.559 billion on December 31, 2024. This points to no meaningful investments made during 2025 so far for PayPal, but given the $6.6 billion cash position, I expect the company to make meaningful investments in the coming years.

A Return To Normal - Valuation And Industry Hype Consolidate

At its height, PayPal enjoyed a 90x earnings multiple. Once the tech bubble burst, it dropped down to a 14/15x earnings multiple. This marks a return to normal for the company and the market in general. I say that as Palantir still trades at over 200x earnings or whatever absurd number it happens to be today. For a business like PayPal this return to normal I believe, is a good thing. I was not an investor in 2021 as I found the multiple way too high. At this level, it is much more attractive as an investment.

Between Q3 2020 and Q2 2025 net income has grown from $3.14 billion to $4.68 billion, translating to 49% growth. FCF margin has grown during the same measuring period from 24.9% to 31.3%.

The stock is currently up 25% from the 52-week lows, but still down 24,6% from the 52-week high of $93 per share. Investors get to buy the stock at a fair valuation, even slightly discounted if you believe PayPal can grow at an accelerated rate compared to market forecasts, pinning PayPal at 5.83% annual revenue growth the next three years.

P/E is currently 50% below the 5-year average for PayPal. The capital leverage which PayPal has is higher than a few years ago, but due to higher EBITDA, it remains manageable. For reference, LT debt is $11.2 billion and TTM EBITDA is $6.5 billion.

Since Alex took over as CEO, ROIC has risen sharply, sitting at 11.46% last quarter. When he took over in 2023 it was at 8.97%. These figures further support my argument that the management shift PayPal underwent is not reflected in the stock price, which is why I am writing this bullish thesis.

TPV Growth Through A Slower, But Steadier Plan

Which direction the share price swings after an earnings release often depends on what the TPV results are, at least from what I've observed the past 2 years. In the second quarter they grew 5%, slightly ahead of 4% in the first quarter.

Under the old CEO (Schulman), PayPal focused much more on aggressive growth, damned be margins and that stuff. This leads to a lot of growth but without a lot of substance unfortunately. Now with a new management style in place the plan is to go more steadily ahead and focus on creating reliable income streams. This is clearly seen as TPV growth has decelerated from a year ago. But, importantly, and I mean important, is that the operating margins have improved. The second quarter they grew 140 bps, first quarter 260 bps (on a yearly basis). This is what I think investors should focus on now. Not that some segment grew in the high teens. I pride myself in investing in value stocks, and these are the exact changes I want to see now for PayPal.

Unbranded checkouts is where much of the growth lies for PYPL now. Offering payment options in apps like Uber or Taco Bell it gathers carve out its own personal space. The launch of a Venmo debit card has also been highly successful growing 40% YoY with monthly active users. The contactless payments options like Pay with Venmo have proven another good investment. I know I used this all the time. I honestly can't remember the last time I pressed in my card code.

The Commoditization Critique: Where PayPal Still Defends Economics

The bear case isn't that PayPal is failing operationallyit's that payments are commoditizing, competitive pressure limits pricing power, and multiples may remain structurally depressed as a result. This deserves a direct response.

PayPal's defensibility sits in three layers. First, the unbranded checkout business (Braintree) is indeed becoming utility-like with compressed margins, but it generates $20+ billion in TPV and serves as customer acquisition for higher-margin services. Second, the branded checkout (PayPal button) retains pricing power because it bundles buyer and seller protections, fraud prevention, and working capital solutions that pure payment rails don't provide. Merchants pay 2.9% + $0.30 not just for transaction processing, but for conversion liftPayPal users complete purchases at 80%+ rates vs 60-70% for guest checkout.

Third, and most defensible, are the value-added services: buy-now-pay-later generating 25%+ net transaction margins, merchant cash advances, and fraud/risk management tools. These businesses are growing faster than core payments and command premium pricing because they solve distinct problems beyond moving money.

The risk is real: if PayPal becomes just another payment rail, multiples stay compressed. But the $2.6 billion in annual "other value added services" revenue growing at 10%+ suggests the company is successfully layering margin-accretive services onto the commodity rails. The question is whether this services mix shift happens fast enough to offset payment commoditizationI believe it does, bears disagree.

Buybacks Reward Long-Term Shareholders

What seems to go under the radar is the large buybacks PayPal continues to make. The past 5 years alone, shares have gone down by 18.4%, or 4.08% annually. During 2025 it has accelerated, going down 7.7% the first 6 months compared to 2024. In June 2022 the management approved a $15 billion buyback plan, which was extended in February 2025 when an additional $15 billion was approved to allocate for this. Last quarter, PayPal bought back shares for roughly $1.4 billion at an average price of $67, netting a 2.7% gain as of today's share price. 2025 guidance for share repurchasing remained the same following the latest quarter, standing strong at $6 billion in total. Given the market capitalization of $66 billion, there is another 4-5% in share reduction likely to occur in the last 6 months of the year.

The sustainability question deserves attention: can PayPal maintain $6 billion annual buybacks indefinitely? Free cash flow over the past 12 months was $6.8 billion, suggesting the current pace is fully covered by operating cash generation. However, $880 million of that FCF was consumed by working capital investments in the loan origination business. Adjusting for this, core FCF runs closer to $7.7 billion, providing comfortable coverage even if loan growth continues.

The risk is if operating margins compress or loan losses spikeeither scenario would pressure FCF and force buyback normalization to $4-5 billion annually. At that reduced pace, share count reduction slows from 7% to 4-5% annually, lowering EPS growth but not eliminating it. My base case assumes $5-6 billion sustained buybacks through 2029, not $6+ billiona conservative posture given management's $15 billion authorization and strong FCF generation.

Scalability - PayPal Can Still Muster Plenty More Growth

The scalability assessment is anchored in the market report that projects the Payment Processing Solutions sector will reach a $139 billion valuation in 2030. Although one report credits the company with a near 60% market share here, I find the perpetual continuation of that share to be overly optimistic. I have therefore based my calculations on more conservative estimates, ranging between 40% and 50% instead. The net margins used are founded on historical figures combined with growth forecasts.

The numbers shown in the cells represent the net income that would supposedly be generated. As previously established, PYPL has a strong history of executing share buybacks. My assumption includes a reduction in outstanding shares by 10% to 15% by 2030, or 877 million using the mid-range figure. This leads to an EPS range of $3.83 to $6.7, and a P/E range of 17.8 to 10.2. I personally do not believe the company will see its market share drop from 55% to 40% in just five years. My assumption is closer to a 48% to 50% range, coupled with increasing margins. My 2030 projection specifically assumes a 48% market share and 19% margins, putting PYPL at a $5.82 EPS and an 11.9x earnings multiple. I am more than comfortable with this price, which once again serves to underline my bullish conviction.

Cost Of Debt - Solid Cash Generation Reduces Need For Debt Financing

In a NOPLAT and Cost of Debt sensitivity table its visible that PYPL shares are undervalued at the moment. The lowest figures at 5% growth and 3,5% cost of debt giving it a share price of $72. On the high end of growth and current Cost of Debt the price is $80.37 per share. The cost of debt is based on the TTM interest expenses ($420 million) and total debt outstanding ($11.2 billion). Conclusion: the table supports a bullish thesis for PYPL showing upside in every cell.

Multiple Valuation

In my multiple analysis I include the 5.8 EPS I've mentioned earlier. The table shows that number in relation to different earnings multiples and a what if case that margins go beyond my assumption. The range I'm comfortable buying a fintech company like PYPL in is between 14 - 18 which is a price range of $81 - $105. At both ends its above current trading value. Once again this module supports my thesis.

Concluding the valuation segment I want to include a price range I believe in. The first one about scalability provides me with a base line for EPS assumptions, $5.7 - $5.9. The second one has me at $80 based on minimum 10% NOPLAT growth and that the cost of debt remains the same at 3.7%. The last one is a range of $81 - $105. Depending on the market environment I can see it reaching the higher end here. But for the sake of giving a more precise range I will stick with $80 - $90 by 2029. This implies an upside of 15.1% - 29.5%.

The Full Picture

PayPal operates as a large-scale online fintech enterprise serving many millions of customers. It currently holds a majority share of the market and consistently pursues internal improvements regarding margins. I now find the investments being directed into Venmo to be completely logical. That is precisely where growth is occurring, and I believe it is where PayPal should concentrate the majority of its resources as well.

If you remain unconvinced that the company can achieve solid EPS growth annually, simply observe the scale of their buybacks. It is truly incredible. Common shares have decreased by 7.7% in just six months, which is absurd for a mature entity like PYPL. You could literally be looking at returns in the low-teens every year if they sustain this pace. Yet, some claim PYPL is a poor investment. The numbers speak for themselves; they are solid across the entire business. The stock clearly exudes value, currently trading down 21% year-to-date.

For an extended period, bears contended that PayPal should be valued similarly to traditional bank stocks. It is effectively valued that way now, trading at 12 times forward earnings. This represents a massive discount compared to JP Morgan (JPM) and Goldman Sachs (GS), which are considered the gold standards for bank valuations. I simply do not grasp the reasoning for why PayPal should be valued lower than these institutions when its growth trajectory is notably better. Until someone presents me with compelling evidence to justify this discrepancy, I will continue to suggest there is upside for PayPal still.

data.title: ATRO: Revenue approaches $1B as innovation, acquisitions, and defense wins drive growth and margins
data.description:
Innovation and strategic acquisitions have positioned the company for growth, with 2025 revenue nearing $1 billion and strong operating leverage driving margin expansion. New convertible notes and repurchases have improved liquidity and minimized dilution, while major defense and aerospace contracts support future performance.

Original document: Astronics Corporation [ATRO] Slides Release — Jan. 14 2026
